Rough draft of the cover of the 2008 version of good kid, m.A.A.d city described and drawn by Kendrick in the Kendrick Lamar Watts 2008 video made by mister fifou, who was contacted by Kendrick to shoot the photos for the cover, to which he declined. Described by Kendrick to be 'basically an irony', the cover would have been a photo of Kendrick sitting on the front porch of a house smiling with a sign in his hand that read 'Peace in compton', he would have had a black eye and a piru and blood member throwing up their gang signs on either side of him. Funnily enough he wrote 'good good madd city' in the drawing instead of the album title. A higher quality version of the drawing is available in fifou's book 'Archives'

OG Filename: 20100904-KENDRICKOD
The album cover art was revealed to the public on September 4, 2010, and was designed by 2DopeBoyz featuring Hassana Lynne and Dave Free. The cover features pop culture icons such as Michael Jackson, Jim Morrison, Bruce Lee, ODB, Jimi Hendrix, Dash Snow, Sacer, and Pimp C. All of these artists overdosed, or OD’d, from one substance or another, fitting with the project’s initials. The woman in the center of the image is sitting in a Sukhasana position; a pose commonly associated with meditation.
